login.title: Sign In
login.subtitle: Access your surveillance platform
login.errorBackend: Cannot reach server — start services with: bash scripts/start-linux.sh (WSL)
login.support: If you experience connection issues, contact technical support:

tutorial.title: Getting started
tutorial.subtitle: Set up CitéVision in 3 guided steps.

cameras.title: 1. Connect your cameras
cameras.desc: Add IP/RTSP cameras with automatic network discovery, then verify the live stream.

zones.title: 2. Draw detection zones
zones.desc: Outline areas and crossing lines on each camera so the AI focuses on what matters.

rules.title: 3. Create smart rules
rules.desc: Combine zones, schedules and detections to trigger alerts and automations (e-mail, webhooks, n8n…).

cameras.title: Camera Management
cameras.emptyHint: Add your first camera using the setup wizard
cameras.deleteConfirmMessage: "{{name}}" will be permanently removed. The camera can be unplugged — deletion does not require it to be online.

wizard.connectionFailed: Cannot display video — check credentials and that the camera is on the same network
wizard.previewFailed: Cannot display video — check credentials and that the camera is on the same network
wizard.scanError: Network scan failed — retry or enter the RTSP URL manually
wizard.scanTimeout: Scan timed out — retry or enter the RTSP URL manually
wizard.scanAuthError: Session expired — sign in again and retry the scan
wizard.scanRateLimit: Too many scans — wait a few seconds
wizard.createError: Failed to create camera — retry or contact support
wizard.missingSite: Site not found — sign out and sign in again
wizard.noDevices: No devices found on this subnet
wizard.manualUrlHint: Use this if your camera is not on the local network or was not detected by the scan.
wizard.vendorHint: Helps optimize the connection path. Choose Generic if your brand is not listed.

vendorCoverage.generic: Partial — uses standard RTSP paths; provide the full URL if connection fails

wizard.helpText: Hikvision and Dahua cameras are fully supported. For other brands (Axis, Reolink, Uniview, etc.), enter the RTSP URL manually from the camera's configuration interface.
wizard.scanHint: The scanner probes common RTSP ports (554, 8554, 10554, 5544, 7070, 8000) then port 80. RTSP badges mark likely video streams — scanning a /24 takes about 10–20 seconds.

users.title: Users
users.emptyHint: Users will appear here once created

rules.title: Analytics Rules
rules.emptyHint: Browse the catalog to activate your first rule
rules.activeSubtitle: Manage, edit and duplicate your active rules
rules.disableError: Failed to disable — check your permissions.

guide.bannerTitle: Pick a rule that fits your context
guide.national: Large-scale monitoring: crowds, traffic, public sites.
guide.enterprise: Private sites: entrances, sensitive zones, vehicles.
guide.domestic: Home or residence: intrusion, presence, access.
guide.all: Browse by scale or configure your active rules below.

catalogFilter.partialExplain: These rules need one extra step (module or calibration). Tap « What's needed » on the card.

catalogNav.lead: What do you want to watch?

rules.catalogFooter: {{total}} rules · {{operational}} ready now · {{partial}} need extra setup

catalogMega.placesHint: Unauthorized entry, exit, or crossing a restricted area.
catalogMega.peopleHint: Presence, unusual behavior, crowds or gatherings.
catalogMega.roadHint: Speeding, congestion, red lights and violations.
catalogMega.identityHint: Known face, person of interest, or saved license plate.
catalogMega.objectsHint: Bag left behind, missing object, accident or emergency.
catalogMega.cameraHint: Is the camera filming well? When should rules be active?

catalogSub.intrusionHint: Someone enters a protected area or loiters near a door.
catalogSub.zonesHint: Enter, exit or stay in a zone you drew.
catalogSub.passagesHint: Cross a line, wrong way, or blocked access.
catalogSub.presenceHint: Someone stays too long, wanders or stops moving.
catalogSub.gesturesHint: Running, falling, fighting, climbing or suspicious gesture.
catalogSub.crowdHint: Too many people, queue, panic or gathering.
catalogSub.speedHint: Vehicle too fast or sudden stop.
catalogSub.trafficHint: Congestion, stopped vehicle or wrong lane.
catalogSub.road_infractionsHint: Red light, seatbelt, phone while driving…
catalogSub.platesHint: Automatic plate reading for allow/block lists.
catalogSub.objectsHint: Abandoned, removed or new object in the scene.
catalogSub.emergenciesHint: Accident, fight, vandalism or critical situation.
catalogSub.image_qualityHint: Blurry or dark image — is the camera working?
catalogSub.schedulesHint: Activate the rule at set times or after a delay.

studio.conditionsHint: Add or remove conditions before the alert triggers.
studio.saveError: Failed to save — check your permissions and configuration.
studio.classRequired: Choose the monitored object type (person, vehicle…) for reliable detection.
studio.evidenceHint: On each alert: a short video clip and two photos (overview + zoomed target) are saved as evidence.
studio.evidenceEnabled: Capture clip + images on each trigger
studio.clipDurationHint: Between 3 and 15 seconds. Default 6 s — enough to understand the situation.
studio.subjectZoomHint: Zooms in on the detected person or object (colored bounding box).
studio.drawBbox: Show detection box on the target image
studio.imageCountHint: Default 2: overview + close-up on target. You can keep one or add a third.
studio.subjectPaddingHint: Space left around the person or object in the zoomed photo.
studio.minConfidenceHint: Ignores uncertain detections before saving evidence.
studio.outputsTitle: What happens when the rule triggers?
studio.outputsHint: In-app alert + optional notifications to email or automation tools (n8n, Make, Zapier…).
studio.notifyEmailHint: An email alert will be sent if the mail server is configured in Settings.
studio.enableWebhook: Also send to an automation webhook
studio.scheduleHint: The rule only triggers during this daily time window. Leave as-is for 24/7 monitoring.
studio.sequenceHint: Steps must occur in order within a time window.

visual.subtitle: Each row is a plain-language condition. The grey line below shows the technical detail.
visual.andExplain: All conditions below must be true at the same time on the same event.
visual.orExplain: At least one condition below is enough to trigger the alert.
visual.sequenceExplain: Steps must happen in order within the configured time window.

simulator.hint: Fictional scenarios to understand when the rule fires or not.
simulator.whyYes: All required conditions are met for this event.
simulator.whyNo: At least one condition is not met — no alert.

guide.fallbackBody: Follow the steps to configure camera, conditions, and actions.

studio.step1Why: Choose the camera and rule-specific settings (zone, duration, object class…)
studio.step1What: These settings define when and where the rule is active.
studio.step2Why: Review trigger logic — this is evaluated on every event.
studio.step2What: Use AND, OR, or Sequence for simple or complex scenarios.
studio.step3Why: Define what happens when the rule fires: alert, recording, notification.
studio.step3What: Combine actions and pick alert severity.
studio.step4What: After activation, the rule monitors the live stream.

group.andIntro: All of these must be true at the same time:
group.orIntro: At least one of these situations is enough:
group.sequenceIntro: These steps must occur in order:

activation.successDesc: Your rule is monitoring {context}. The first alert should arrive within 30 seconds if an event is detected.
activation.watchingDesc: The rule is active. The system is checking the video stream.
activation.checkZone: Is a zone or line drawn on the camera?
activation.checkCamera: Is the camera online and the RTSP stream active?
activation.checkAi: Is the AI engine running (green indicator in System Health)?
activation.checkActivity: Is there activity in the camera field of view?

alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.emptyHint: Alerts will appear here in real time

events.title: Event Timeline
events.selectHint: Select an event to view evidence

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.none: No structured evidence for this item.
evidence.subject: Detected subject
evidence.automationsHint: Channels configured on the source rule at the time of the alert.
evidence.forwardHint: Send alert data and evidence links to an email or automation tool (n8n, Make, Zapier…).
evidence.forwardEmailHint: A summary with evidence links will be sent if the mail server is configured.
evidence.forwardWebhookHint: Full alert data and evidence snapshot will be sent as JSON.
evidence.forwardError: Forward failed — check email, URL or SMTP configuration.

liveView.title: Live View
liveView.streamError: Cannot display video — check credentials and that the camera is on the same network

videoWall.title: Video Wall
videoWall.emptyHint: Add cameras for the video wall

zoneEditor.title: Zone Editor
zoneEditor.emptyHint: Draw detection zones on the canvas
zoneEditor.noLinesHint: Switch to line mode and draw a crossing line on the camera view.
zoneEditor.noCameraHint: Select a camera from the list above to start drawing.
zoneEditor.instructionsZone: Click on the video to place points. Minimum 3 points, then « Close polygon » and Save.
zoneEditor.instructionsLine: Click to set the start point, then the end point of the line, then Save.
zoneEditor.loadFailed: Failed to load zones — check connection.
zoneEditor.saveError: Draw at least one zone (3 clicks, then «Close polygon»).
zoneEditor.lineSaveError: Draw at least one line before saving.
zoneEditor.zoneKindTip: Semantic type tells the AI how to interpret this zone. Perimeter → intrusion. Controlled exit → unauthorized exit alert. Parking → illegal if vehicle is stationary. Auto = inferred from zone name.
zoneEditor.behaviorTip: Choose what the AI should do with this zone: standard detection, presence, perimeter, traffic-light color, speed measurement, phone/seatbelt model, etc. Each behavior shows its reliability level and settings.
zoneEditor.redLightSynergyWarning: This behavior requires a « Traffic light color » (traffic_light_color) zone on the same camera to detect red.
zoneEditor.renameHint: Give a clear name to remember this zone's role. Press Enter or click outside to save.
zoneEditor.renameFailed: Could not save the name — check connection.

settings.title: Settings
settings.toursAutoStart: Launch automatically when arriving on a page

audit.title: Audit Log
audit.emptyHint: User actions will be logged here

systemHealth.title: System Health
systemHealth.modelMissingHint: This model is required for identity-related rules to work. See docs/INSTALL.md for setup instructions.
systemHealth.aiUnreachable: AI engine unreachable — model status cannot be checked.

system.startModeHint: Automatic: starts at Windows logon (scheduled tasks) or Linux boot (systemd). Manual: start-citevision.bat or systemctl.
system.startModeUacHint: Changing mode reconfigures Windows scheduled tasks (no services.msc).
system.startModeConfirmAuto: Enable automatic startup with the system? The currently running application is not stopped.
system.startModeConfirmManual: Switch to manual startup? CitéVision will not start automatically after reboot. The currently running application is not stopped.
system.startModeGuideAutoBody: Windows: CiteVision-AutoStart (at logon) and CiteVision-Watchdog (every 3 min, restarts if the app stops). Linux: systemd enable citevision.service (boot). After a PC reboot, CitéVision starts without manual action.
system.startModeGuideManualBody: Windows: run start-citevision.bat at the project root; no automatic restart after reboot or shutdown. Linux: systemctl start citevision or scripts/start-linux.sh; systemd is not enabled at boot.
system.startModeGuideWindowsNote: Changing mode does not affect the current session: the application stays running. Start / Stop buttons only launch or shut down the stack immediately. Auto-restart (watchdog) only applies in automatic mode.
system.startModeGuideLinuxNote: Start / Stop buttons control citevision.service. In automatic mode the unit is enabled at boot; in manual mode you must start explicitly after each reboot.
system.startModeGuideStatusNote: “Startup configured: Yes” means your mode choice was saved and applied (scheduled tasks, Windows registry, or Startup folder). Mode switching cannot fail: the preference is always persisted.
system.serviceNotRegistered: Automatic startup not configured. Re-run setup.bat or switch mode here.
system.serviceNotRegisteredCta: Windows: automatic mode creates logon scheduled tasks (watchdog every 3 min). If missing, re-run installation or change mode above. Manual: double-click start-citevision.bat at project root. Linux: systemctl enable citevision.
system.serviceNeedsRepairCta: The citevision systemd unit needs repair. Re-run: sudo bash installer/linux/install-service.sh
system.actionConfirmStart: Start CitéVision? All services (backend, AI, UI) will be launched.
system.actionConfirmStop: Stop CitéVision? All services will be shut down.
system.actionUacHint: Mode changes reconfigure scheduled tasks (Windows) or systemd (Linux).

uninstall.title: Uninstall
uninstall.description: Removes the system service, stops all CitéVision processes and tears down Docker infrastructure. Irreversible.
uninstall.keepDataHint: Preserves Docker volumes (PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO…) — only removes binaries and services.
uninstall.wipeWarning: By default, all Docker volumes will be deleted. Organization data will be lost.
uninstall.confirmWipe: This will remove the service, stop all processes and wipe Docker volumes. Irreversible.
uninstall.confirmKeepData: This will remove the service and stop all processes. Docker volumes will be kept.
uninstall.inProgress: Please wait — do not close this window…
uninstall.reinstallHint: Run setup.bat (Windows) or bash scripts/setup-wsl.sh to reinstall.

errorState.title: Failed to load
errorState.hint: Could not fetch data from the API
errorState.backendUnavailableHint: The API is restarting or briefly unreachable. Try again in a few seconds — your session is preserved.

dashboard.statsDesc: Cameras, alerts, events and rules — hover for explanations.
dashboard.alertsDesc: Latest alerts with link to details and evidence.
dashboard.liveDesc: AI-detected events live on your cameras.

map.modesDesc: Real OSM map, schematic plan, or 3D globe.
map.canvasDesc: Hover over a camera for live preview; click for details.

rules.catalogDesc: Filter by scale (national, enterprise, domestic) and activate an illustrated rule.
rules.activeDesc: Only rules you created — activate or disable with the dedicated button.

alerts.filtersDesc: Filter by period, camera, rule and severity. Each alert can include visual evidence.

videoWall.layoutDesc: Choose 1, 4, 9, or 16 cameras on the wall.
videoWall.gridDesc: Each quadrant fills the space without overlap.

liveView.playerDesc: Select a camera and control PTZ — the stream is the source of detections.

cameras.listDesc: Add and test your cameras here.

zones.canvasDesc: Draw zones and detection lines on the camera view.

demoCenter.title: CitéVision Demonstration
demoCenter.subtitle: Video, zoning, rules and alerts — ready for client presentation.
demoCenter.step1Body: Looping test stream. LIVE badge = ready.
demoCenter.step2Body: Expand the panel under the video: 3 clicks → Close polygon → Save.
demoCenter.step3Body: Catalog below → Configure. The rule monitors the zone you drew.
demoCenter.step4Body: Live results (30–60 s after activation). Each alert includes video evidence.
demoCenter.emptyDetections: Waiting… let the video run 30–60 s.
demoCenter.emptyAlertes: Activate a catalog rule to receive alerts.
demoCenter.transcodeHint: Large files may take 2–3 minutes. Do not close the page.
demoCenter.noVideos: No videos — upload an MP4 to get started.
demoCenter.largeFileWarning: Large file (>500 MB) — conversion may take several minutes.
demoCenter.zoneInlineHint: 3 clicks on the video → Close polygon → Save.
demoCenter.zoneInlineNeedStream: Upload a test video or select a camera to draw zones.
demoCenter.selectSourceBeforeZones: Select a video source explicitly before editing zones.
demoCenter.navLabelHint: Name shown in the sidebar menu
demoCenter.emptyStreamTitle: Upload an MP4 to get started
demoCenter.emptyStreamBody: No active stream. Upload a test video (max 5) to start the demo.
demoCenter.resetConfirm: Clear all test data (events, alerts, demo rules)?
demoCenter.mailhogTip: Open MailHog to preview alert emails sent during the demo
demoCenter.lineCountersEmpty: No crossings counted yet. Draw a counting line and enable a counting rule.
demoCenter.streamUnavailable: Video stream unavailable — upload a test video or select an active source.
